label: Other
rationale: Not applicable: the model predicted 2024 while the gold year is 2010 (prediction > gold), so this is not a non-conservative (underestimate) error. The mismatch direction is the opposite of the scenario in the prompt; therefore none of the extraction/search buckets fit.

label: Fail to extract the right entities
rationale: The model mis-extracted temporal information: the passage explicitly contains 1878 (Morgan dollar) and 2017 (Ballon d'Or source), but the model assigned an explicit_year/estimate of 2001. Its justification even cites 1878 while its entity fields and final estimate are 2001, indicating an error in extracting or mapping the correct entity-year pairs rather than a retrieval or query issue.

label: Fail to extract the right entities
rationale: The model appears to have ignored or failed to extract the explicit publication year of the review (2018) and instead based its estimate on years of cited experiments/papers (e.g., 2010–2013). In other words, the review entity with an explicit 2018 timestamp was not picked up as the primary temporal signal, so the model reverted to older citation dates and produced an earlier prediction.

label: Fail to extract the right entities
rationale: The model anchored on FINRA (explicit 2007) as the latest explicit year and omitted the 2017 entity (the book "Autentico: Cooking Italian, the Authentic Way") that sets the gold year to 2017. In other words, it failed to extract the correct/latest entity from the sample that would have produced the 2017 date.

label: Fail to extract the right entities
rationale: The model treated the sample as ‘timeless’ and did not extract the explicit time-anchored entity present in the gold data (the LLM refusal tied to GPT‑3 in 2020). By failing to detect that explicit entity/year, it defaulted to an earlier estimate (2001) instead of using the 2020 evidence.
